event: born in a country with a high burden of tuberculosis
in the care of a tertiary care infectious disease clinic for TB infection
not taken corticosteroids for several years
began treatment with 600 mg rifampin (10 mg/kg/d)
normal physical examination aside from arthritis in 2 joints
ambulance transport to the emergency department
admitted to the intensive care unit
leukocyte count increased to 17.34 × 10^9/L 6 hours after ED admission
blood film did not show schistocytes
C-reactive protein (CRP) level of 13.6 (normal < 1.7) mg/L at admission
C-reactive protein level increased to 101.8 mg/L about 24 hours later
aspartate aminotransferase level was 71 (normal < 31) U/L at admission
aspartate aminotransferase level increased to 99 U/L during the first 24 hours
complement C3 level was slightly decreased (0.76 g/L)
C4 level was within normal range
previous finding of a partially empty sella turcica on magnetic resonance imaging
CT scan of the head was normal
CT angiography of the neck did not show an infected thrombus
CT angiography of the neck did not show edematous retropharyngeal soft tissue
CT angiography of the neck showed localized myositis
chest radiograph showed mild pulmonary edema attributed to fluid resuscitation
mild pulmonary edema responded to diuresis
CT imaging of the lungs showed bilateral increased septal thickening
CT imaging of the lungs showed ground glass opacities
serology for Epstein-Barr virus was negative
received 1 dose of rifampin in the ICU
decreased blood pressure of 93/39 mm Hg
attributed her presentation to a rifampin-induced adverse drug reaction
started her on isoniazid for TBI
told her to avoid rifamycin-based medications in the future
pattern of symptoms beginning a few hours after rifampin ingestion
vasopressor dose remained increased for 6 hours
pattern of symptoms resolving a few hours later
